settings.text: Every inquiry is read by one of the founders — Mira or Alex — usually within a business day. If your project is a fit for the studio, we'll come back to you with follow-up questions and a time to talk. If it's not, we'll say so honestly and often point you toward a studio that is.

settings.title: What you're building
settings.description: A paragraph is plenty. Product name, what it does, who it's for, roughly where you are.

settings.title: Timeline
settings.description: When you'd want to start, when you'd want to ship. Rough windows are fine.

settings.title: Rough budget
settings.description: A range, a ceiling, or "not sure yet" — whatever you have. It helps us figure out whether this is a sprint, a project, or something else.

settings.title: Current stack
settings.description: What's already in place — if anything. Languages, frameworks, hosting, whatever's relevant.

settings.title: What's blocking you
settings.description: The specific thing you want help with. Shipping faster, hiring more slowly, fixing a rewrite that stalled — anything.

settings.title: After you write.
settings.description: We keep the process deliberate — every inquiry gets a real read and a real reply.

settings.title: We read every inquiry
settings.description: One of the founders reads what you wrote and decides how to respond. No automated funnel, no discovery call with someone who'll hand you off later.

settings.title: Reply within two business days
settings.description: Sometimes sooner. You'll hear back with follow-up questions and — if there's a likely fit — a time to jump on a call.

settings.title: Scoping call
settings.description: Forty-five minutes on video. A real technical conversation about your product, your constraints, and whether we're the right studio. No slides, no pitch.

settings.title: Still have a question?
settings.text:
The Services page has more detail on pricing, engagement modes, and what each includes.

Or skip straight to the inquiry — the scoping call is the fastest way to get a real answer to a specific question.

settings.question: How soon will we hear back?
settings.answer: Within two business days, almost always. If we're in the middle of a project kickoff week it may be three — but you'll always hear from a human, not an auto-reply.

settings.question: What if we're too small or too early?
settings.answer: Then we'll say so, and often point you toward a friend of the studio — a freelancer or a smaller shop who's better set up for early-stage work. Honest referrals save everyone time.

settings.question: Do you sign NDAs before the call?
settings.answer: Rarely. The discovery call is high-level, and we've never had a situation where we needed to see something confidential before knowing if we're a fit. If your project genuinely needs one, send ours or yours and we'll sign before the call.

settings.question: Can we skip the email and just book a call?
settings.answer: We prefer a written inquiry first — it forces the conversation to be specific and lets the founder on your project come to the call prepared. The three business days from inquiry to call is time well spent.
